package.name: odometry_filter_node
package.description:
Lifecycle-managed ROS 2 node that drives the OdometryFilter (provided by the odometry_filter package). Subscribes to /imu, /motor_rpm, /steering_angle, /brake_pressure; publishes /odom plus /odom_diag/* diagnostics; broadcasts odom→base_link TF.
Pre-#431 this work lived inside sim_supervisor_node.py as a Python callback path. That path was at ~93 % CPU on a single core because rclpy dispatches the BMI088 400 Hz IMU callback 400 times per second with Python overhead. Same algorithm in rclcpp drops the overhead by ~25-50x per call. Phase 1 (#431) shipped the pure-math library + tests; this package is the Phase 2 ROS wrapper. Phase 3 strips the Python OdometryFilter from sim_supervisor_node and flips this node on by default.
Lifecycle-managed because the rest of the DV pipeline already is — the diagram contract treats /odom availability as a lifecycle-gated capability, not always-on.
